Title: Heat wave smashes records across central US
Article:
WASHINGTON: A record-breaking heat wave baked the central United States on Sunday, smashing temperature records from the northern plains to the Rocky Mountains region.
In Salt Lake City, the state capital of Utah, and Billings, the largest city in Montana, temperatures reached a peak of 43C, according to preliminary data from the US National Weather Service.
Both temperatures are all-time highs for each city since records started being kept over 150 years ago, surpassing the previous records of 42C.
The blistering heat has also hindered efforts to fight massive wildfires raging in Colorado and Utah, and the high temperatures are expected to persist through Tuesday.
Just over a week ago, the eastern United States was gripped by another heat wave that pushed temperatures to around 40C in New York and Philadelphia.
Around the world, heat waves are becoming more common and intense due to climate change, primarily caused by the burning of coal, oil and gas and emission of greenhouse gases.
Western Europe experienced its hottest June on record.
The heat wave left more than 1,300 people dead across the region, according to the World Health Organisation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid to meet Nga over resignation remarks after BN's Johor win
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said he will meet Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming after the latter's pledge to resign if Barisan Nasional (BN) secured a major victory in the Johor election.
Zahid, who is also Umno president, said such remarks might be expected from the opposition but were unnecessary among parties serving in the same unity government.
"I do not want to advise him (to resign), but such statements should be made appropriately.
"We meet every week during Cabinet meetings. If we were in the opposition, perhaps such statements could be considered appropriate.
"But if we are in the same government, there is no need for such matters to be raised. It's okay, I will meet him. He is my friend," he told reporters after officiating the TVET@KKDW contingent heading to the WorldSkills Shanghai 2026 competition here today.
Asked about federal-level ties following BN's victory in the Johor election, Zahid said relations within the unity government remained very good.
"Earlier, I also met other friends, and we remained professional.
"Actually, under the unity government, ministers and deputy ministers are operating in a very conducive environment.
"We are moving as one team in this unity government in a professional manner. That is our hope because we promised to remain in this unity government until the end of the term," he said.
Zahid added that the reality of the relationship between the ruling parties differed from public perception, as ties among coalition partners remained strong.
Nga was previously reported to have said that he would be the first minister to resign if Umno secured a landslide victory in the polls and former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak was released.
However, Nga later clarified that his resignation remark was conditional on former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak being
released
without serving a sentence commensurate with his offence.
On July 11, BN won 48 of the 56 seats up for grabs in the Johor election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: EU countries weigh trade ban on Israeli settlements
Article:
BRUSSELS: EU foreign ministers on Monday discussed imposing an import ban on products from Israeli settlements, after pressure from a raft of member states.
"Everybody agrees that the situation in the West Bank is really intolerable,"  EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said at the start of a meeting in Brussels.
"What is happening in the West Bank is actually making it more and more impossible that the two-state solution ever can come into effect."
Several EU countries – including Ireland, the Netherlands, and Spain – have already imposed their own trade restrictions on Israeli settlements in the occupied Palestinian territories, considered illegal under international law.
Under pressure for the EU as a whole to take measures, the bloc's executive last week laid out options to curb trade with settlements, including a ban.
"There have been a lot of asks and requests from the member states regarding the ban of the trade with illegal settlements," Kallas said.
"Let's see if these options that have been provided now will have a stronger push from member states."
Diplomats said the debate at a meeting in Brussels on Monday was not expected to yield any concrete decisions, but would help to sound out if there is enough support to move forward.
The slow pace of the discussion has angered countries keen to curb trade – with some diplomats accusing the European Commission of dragging its feet.
Belgium's foreign minister Maxime Prevot said the options laid out appeared to be more "a bone to gnaw on than a genuine desire to move forward."
"We are calling for concrete proposals," he said.
There is disagreement in Brussels as to whether that move would need backing from all 27 member states or just a weighted majority.
Diplomats say that key players Germany and Italy are still undecided on the move.
Israel has occupied the West Bank since 1967. More than 500,000 Israeli settlers live in the territory, excluding east Jerusalem, among some three million Palestinians.
The UN chief Antonio Guterres has condemned the "relentless" expansion of Israeli settlements in the West Bank, saying in a report seen by AFP last month that they are contributing to the territory's worst displacement crisis since 1967.
The EU has long been hampered by divisions over its approach towards Israel, with some members staunchly backing the country and others supporting the Palestinians.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid: BN's Negri Sembilan strategy tailored to local landscape
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional (BN) will adopt a formula tailored to Negri Sembilan's political landscape when determining its candidates for the state election.
BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the approach would not focus solely on candidates, but also take into account factors unique to the state.
"This is not just about candidates. The situation in Negri Sembilan is certainly different because the number of seats, population and voter demographics are different.
"So, the formula must be adjusted according to the political situation in Negri Sembilan. We will announce the candidates this week," he told reporters after officiating the TVET@KKDW contingent heading to the WorldSkills Shanghai 2026 competition here today.
Yesterday, BN secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said the coalition would apply the election "formula" used in its
recent landslide victory in Johor
to the upcoming Negri Sembilan polls.
Zambry said the strategy focused on prudence, discipline and stability while rejecting the politics of hatred and slander.
The 16th Negri Sembilan election is scheduled for Aug 1, with nomination day set for July 18. All 36 state seats will be contested.
In the 2023 election, BN won 14 seats, while Perikatan Nasional secured five. Pakatan Harapan won the remaining 17 seats, with DAP taking 11, PKR five and Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) one.
BN retained its position in the 16th Johor election held two days ago.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'No agreement with Pas': Zahid dismisses seat talks claim
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno has no agreement with Pas on seat negotiations for the Negri Sembilan election, says Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
Responding to Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang's claim that talks between the two parties were ongoing, Zahid said any discussions were merely based on an understanding and had yet to result in a final agreement.
He said not all statements from Pas could be taken as accurate, describing Hadi's remarks as a political statement by the party.
"Not everything that has been said should be accepted as an accurate statement because we do not have any agreement with Pas.
"Even if there have been discussions, they are merely an understanding, and we have not finalised anything," he told reporters after officiating the TVET@KKDW contingent heading to the WorldSkills Shanghai 2026 competition here today.
Zahid also cautioned against placing too much reliance on Pas, saying the party had changed its position on previous occasions.
"We should not rely too much on them because they can change. This has happened many times before, and many parties have experienced this with them.
"Let's see how things go because in Johor, what they said was different from the reality before nomination day and even on polling day," he said.
He added that while Pas could be given the benefit of the doubt, its position could still change.
"They can change the goalposts at any time. So I view that as their political statement," he said.
Earlier, Hadi said Pas
was prepared to accept
an Umno leader as the Negri Sembilan menteri besar candidate if cooperation between the two parties was finalised for the Aug 1 state election.
He said seat negotiations were ongoing, with discussions including Umno retaining its 14 seats and Pas keeping its three existing seats.
The two seats previously held by Bersatu would be allocated to either Pas or Umno.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'We can finally prepare for the wedding,' says Syed Saddiq's mother
Article:
https://www.bharian.com.my/berita/nasional/2026/07/1587252/kami-akan-buat-persiapan-kahwin-syed-saddiq-dan-bella-biarlah⁠
PUTRAJAYA: Wedding preparations for Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman and singer-actress Bella Astillah can finally begin after the Federal Court ruled in his favour today.
His mother, Sharifah Mahani Syed Abdul Aziz, said the family had put their wedding plans on hold until the court delivered its decision.
"We really couldn't make any preparations because we were waiting for today's decision.
"So now that the decision has gone in Syed Saddiq's favour, I am overjoyed. Imagine if the outcome had been otherwise today," she said.
Sharifah Mahani said she had also refrained from revealing the wedding date, as she wanted to wait until the case had been resolved.
"I did not want to disappoint someone else's daughter. That would have weighed heavily on my conscience," she said.
She said the family could now focus on the couple's wedding preparations. The nuptials are expected to take place at the end of the year.
"Now we can even start preparing the bunga telur. Before this, we simply were not in the mood because of the court case.
"This case did not last for just a year or two. It has gone on for a long time. Now, let this happiness be theirs," she said.
Sharifah Mahani also admitted she had not been able to sleep or eat while awaiting the court's decision.
"I really could not sleep or eat. People say those in love forget to bathe, but this was something else entirely," she said.
She was also grateful that her son had been surrounded by people who loved and understood the sacrifices he had made, particularly Bella, whom she described as being steadfast by his side throughout his legal ordeal.
"It has not been easy for anyone to stand by Syed Saddiq while he had to keep going back and forth to court.
"I often wondered whether he would ever meet someone willing to make those sacrifices and go through all this with him.
"Alhamdulillah, Bella was willing," she said.
Earlier, the Federal Court upheld Syed Saddiq's appeal, acquitting the Muar member of parliament and former youth and sports minister of four charges involving criminal breach of trust, dishonest misappropriation of property and money laundering linked to Bersatu Youth funds, overturning his earlier conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bangladeshi man breaks down as Penang raids net 21 undocumented migrants
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A Bangladeshi  broke down in tears, pleading that he had already bought a ticket to return home, as Immigration officers detained 21 foreigners during raids on four business premises here today.
Penang Immigration director Syamsul Fitry Ahmad said the operation, which began at 9am, targeted premises suspected of employing or being operated by foreigners based on public complaints and intelligence.
A total of 35 people were screened, with 21 foreigners detained for various immigration offences.
"The operation targeted business premises based on complaints and intelligence gathered. We checked four premises and detained 21 foreigners for various immigration offences," he said after the operation at Jalan Datuk Keramat here today.
Checks at one of the premises, an eatery, found it abandoned, with food still cooking on the stove.
The operation also drew curious onlookers.
Those detained comprised 15 Indian men, three Bangladeshi men and three Myanmar men, aged between 18 and 51.
Syamsul Fitry said the Bangladeshi man cried and pleaded with officers during the operation, claiming he worked at a hotel and had gone to one of the shops to buy goods.
"Based on initial investigations, we found some inconsistencies. We will check with the hotel concerned to verify the man's claims," he said.
Syamsul Fitry said several detainees claimed they were merely shopping when approached by enforcement officers, but preliminary investigations indicated some were working as shop assistants.
He said statements would be recorded from the detainees and their employers before any action was taken.
"Among the offences were failing to produce valid identification documents, breaching pass conditions, overstaying, holding unrecognised identification cards and other offences under the Immigration Act.
"Initial checks also found some foreigners had overstayed in Malaysia for as long as three years after entering on social visit passes," he said.
He said all 21 detainees would be sent to the Jawi Immigration Depot for further investigation and action.
The department also issued five Form 29 witness summonses to Malaysians to assist in investigations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Immigration mulls higher fines for hiring undocumented workers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department may propose higher fines for employers who hire undocumented foreign workers if the current maximum compound of RM10,000 per worker fails to act as a deterrent.
Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said many employers remained undeterred and continued to hire undocumented migrants despite continuous nationwide enforcement.
"If the current rate is still not effective, we will study the matter and may recommend to the government that the compound be increased," he told reporters after leading an Ops Mega enforcement operation in Jalan Kenanga.
Zakaria said employers often cite the high cost of legal recruitment, including processing fees, transportation and other expenses, as the reason they turn to undocumented migrants.
"With illegal workers, they only need to pay wages, which are also usually lower than those employed legally," he said.
He urged employers to regularise their workforce by either sending undocumented workers home voluntarily under the Migrant Repatriation Programme or applying for legal foreign worker quotas through the Human Resources Ministry.
Zakaria added that the government was discussing stricter enforcement measures involving other agencies, including local authorities and the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry, to curb the practice.
The department detained nearly 37,000 foreigners for
immigration offences in the first six months
of this year following a nationwide crackdown.
A total of 36,900 foreigners were detained in 6,237 enforcement operations conducted between Jan 1 and June 30.
The operations also saw 84,464 individuals screened, while 978 employers were detained for allegedly committing offences under the Immigration Act.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SD Property loses bid for extra WCE land compensation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sime Darby Property Bhd has lost its court bid to retain an additional RM78.6 million in compensation arising from the government's acquisition of its land for the West Coast Expressway (WCE) project.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing on Monday, SD Property said the Shah Alam High Court ruled in favour of the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM), setting aside the RM78.6 million awarded to its wholly owned unit, Sime Darby Property (Klang) Sdn Bhd, for severance and injurious affection.
The court has yet to issue its written grounds of judgment.
The company said it is assessing the High Court's decision, including its legal options and the potential financial impact of the ruling.
The case concerns the compulsory acquisition of seven parcels of land measuring about 504,101 square metres for the WCE project.
In 2015, the Klang district land administrator awarded total compensation of RM169.3 million, comprising RM90.7 million for the land's market value and RM78.6 million for severance and injurious affection.
The latter refers to compensation for losses suffered when the acquisition of part of a property reduces the value or usability of the remaining land.
LLM challenged the RM78.6 million award in the High Court in August 2015.
The dispute went through several rounds of court proceedings and appeals before being reheard on March 10 and 11 this year.
Following last Friday's ruling, the court will hear consequential matters, including legal costs, on July 20.
SD Property said the case is not expected to have any material operational impact on the group at this stage.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] 111 undocumented migrants nabbed in raid on Jalan Kenanga shops
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Immigration officers swept through Jalan Kenanga's wholesale clothing hub today, arresting 111 undocumented migrants during a raid.
Arriving in unmarked vans and cars to avoid detection, enforcement teams moved in about 11am before raiding dozens of shops simultaneously.
The raids sparked chaotic scenes as undocumented migrants tried to escape through back lanes and adjoining buildings after spotting the officers.
Some climbed into attics above shop lots packed with clothing, while others hid among stacks of boxes, on rooftops, and even between display mannequins.
At one premises, officers discovered five foreign workers hiding in a fourth-floor attic after employers allegedly instructed them to conceal themselves there.
[[nid:1487675]]
In another shop, enforcement personnel used grinders to cut through locked metal doors before uncovering several migrants hiding beneath piles of boxes and inside storage areas.
Among those detained was a 27-year-old Bangladeshi man who claimed to be a college student in Kuala Lumpur.
"I don't work here. I only stay upstairs because I'm still studying," he told officers in broken Malay and English.
Another Bangladeshi man also claimed to be pursuing business studies at a local college.
However, he failed to produce a valid student pass when requested and admitted he did not possess one. Both were detained for further investigations.
Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said 236 foreigners were screened during the operation, with 111 found to have committed various immigration offences.
"Those detained comprised 87 men and 24 women aged between 22 and 42. They include six Indonesians, 69 Bangladeshis, one Indian, 31 Myanmar nationals, one Nepalese and three others. They were arrested for offences including not possessing valid travel documents, overstaying and other violations under the Immigration Act," he said.
Zakaria said all detainees would be sent to an immigration detention depot.
He said the operation formed part of the department's ongoing nationwide Ops Mega to track down undocumented migrants and take enforcement action against employers who hire or harbour them illegally.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Banks embrace AI, but trust gap persists: Study
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's banking and development financial institution (DFI) sector is accelerating the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI).
But most lenders remain reluctant to rely on the technology for critical business decisions, reflecting persistent gaps in governance, skills and organisational readiness, according to a study by the Asian Institute of Chartered Bankers (AICB), Ecosystm and the AICB Chief Risk Officers' Forum.
The study also found AI is increasingly being deployed in areas such as Know Your Customer (KYC) onboarding, fraud detection, anti-money laundering, counter financing of terrorism and employee productivity.
However, confidence in AI remains limited, with only 25 per cent of respondents saying they trust AI-generated outputs enough to support key business decisions.
The study also showed that 44 per cent of Malaysian banks and DFIs are at the "developing" stage of AI readiness, having moved beyond pilot projects but still grappling with fragmented capabilities in data, talent and operating models.
Only 15 per cent have reached an established level of AI maturity, while just 2 per cent are considered advanced, where AI is fully embedded into decision-making and business strategy.
The report highlighted several structural challenges.
Only 26 per cent of institutions have a clearly defined AI strategy linked to business objectives, despite 44 per cent already developing customised AI solutions.
Meanwhile, 79 per cent cited shortages of specialised AI talent, while only 20 per cent actively encourage AI-driven decision-making across their organisations.
Governance also remains a key concern. More than half (53 per cent) of respondents still rely on fragmented or ad hoc governance frameworks, while only 33 per cent have established structured AI governance and model risk management practices.
AICB chief executive Edward Ling said the industry has moved beyond debating whether AI belongs in financial services.
"The question now is whether institutions have the judgement, ethics, governance and professional capability to use AI responsibly in decisions that affect customers, risk and institutional performance."
AICB Chief Risk Officers' Forum chairman Dr Chong Han Hwee said AI risks extend well beyond the technology itself.
"The risks emerge across the entire ecosystem, from data quality and human usage patterns to the decisions informed by AI and how these factors evolve over time."
Ecosystm vice president of industry insights Sash Mukherjee said financial institutions are seeking greater clarity on model risk management, explainability, third-party AI and data governance as AI adoption expands.
"But regulation alone will not keep pace with the technology. Ongoing collaboration between industry and regulators will be equally critical to ensure governance frameworks evolve alongside AI innovation."
The study was based on responses from 87 senior executives across commercial banks, digital banks, Islamic banks and DFIs, supplemented by industry interviews and executive roundtable discussions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ops Mega nets 503 undocumented foreigners in nationwide raids
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Immigration Department detained 503 undocumented foreigners in simultaneous raids at 16 locations nationwide under a two-day Ops Mega enforcement operation.
The operation targeted businesses suspected of employing undocumented migrants and those allegedly operated by foreigners.
Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said the nationwide operation, which began yesterday and continued today, involved 876 enforcement personnel from the Immigration Department, National Registration Department (NRD), Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN), local authorities and police.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Prosecution accepts Federal Court's decision to uphold Syed Saddiq's acquittal
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The prosecution in the case involving Muar member of parliament Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman said it respected the Federal Court's decision to
uphold his acquittal
and discharge on corruption-related charges.
Deputy public prosecutor Datuk Wan Shaharuddin Wan Ladin said the prosecution was grateful for the court's decision.
He said it had yet to decide whether to seek a review of the three-member bench's ruling.
"It is still too early to determine the next course of action.
"We will wait for the Attorney-General's decision," Wan Shaharuddin said.
Also appearing for the prosecution was deputy public prosecutor Datuk Ahmad Akram Gharib.
The Federal Court today upheld Syed Saddiq's
acquittal on charges of criminal breach of trust
(CBT), dishonest misappropriation of property and money laundering involving funds linked to Bersatu Youth.
A three-member bench chaired by Court of Appeal president Datuk Seri Abu Bakar Jais dismissed the prosecution's final appeal to reinstate the conviction and sentence, ruling that it lacked merit.
In a 2-1 majority decision, the court held that the prosecution had failed to establish that the Court of Appeal had made any appealable error in overturning the High Court's conviction.
Judge Datuk Collin Lawrence Sequerah, who delivered the majority judgment, agreed with the appellate court's findings that the prosecution had failed to prove the essential elements of the offences.
Sequerah ruled that the two money laundering charges could not stand as they were predicated on the alleged dishonest misappropriation offence.
Judge Datuk Che Mohd Ruzima Ghazali concurred with the majority decision, while Abu Bakar delivered a separate judgment, agreeing that the first charge could not stand but dissenting on the remaining three charges.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sultan Sharafuddin takes LRT3 ride, honours 13,000 workers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, took a ride on the Shah Alam LRT3 Line today from Bandar Utama to Johan Setia, accompanied by Tengku Permaisuri Selangor and the Raja Muda of Selangor.
In a statement from Istana Alam Shah, the ruler congratulated Prasarana Malaysia Bhd on completing the long-awaited line, saying it would help ease congestion and improve public transport connectivity in Shah Alam, Klang and surrounding areas.
"Alhamdulillah, the Shah Alam LRT3 Line is now complete and can be used. I believe there is wisdom that Allah wants to show through this delay," he said, praising Selangor residents for their patience throughout the wait.
The Sultan also paid tribute to more than 13,000 workers, including engineers, technicians, labourers and support staff, who contributed to the project over 10 years and nine months of construction.
"The true success of this project is a manifestation of the dedication, professionalism and spirit of more than 13,000 individuals who devoted their effort and expertise throughout its construction," he said.
He also looked forward to the opening of five additional stations — Tropicana, Temasya, Raja Muda, Bukit Raja and Bandar Botanik — and urged the public to make full use of the service.
The Shah Alam
LRT3 Line
was officially launched by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim on June 28, with full operations beginning the following day.
The
five additional stations
, which were deferred from the initial rollout, will be completed in a later phase.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH to unveil Negri Sembilan election line-up tomorrow night
Article:
SEREMBAN: Pakatan Harapan (PH) will announce its full list of candidates for the Negri Sembilan state election tomorrow night.
According to a post on PH's official Facebook page, the announcement will be made by PH chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim at the PH Negri Sembilan Candidates Announcement Ceremony at Dataran Melang, Kuala Pilah.
Nomination day is set for this Saturday, with
polling scheduled
for Aug 1.
The event is expected to be attended by PH deputy chairmen Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu and Anthony Loke, as well as PH Negri Sembilan chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
Aminuddin, who is also the caretaker Menteri Besar, said PH would contest all 36 state seats on its own, without Barisan Nasional (BN).
"PKR will contest 16 seats, DAP 11 seats, and Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) nine seats," he said.
DAP secretary-general and Negri Sembilan DAP chairman Loke has already confirmed several candidates and key seats, including his own defence of Chennah, which he has won for three consecutive terms.
The state assembly was
dissolved on June 4
after Aminuddin's administration came under pressure following a constitutional crisis involving the Undang institution and Umno's brief withdrawal of support in April.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: FOI Bill to be referred to special select committee for 'comprehensive review'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Freedom of Information (FOI) Bill 2026 will be referred to a Parliamentary Special Select Committee for further scrutiny following its first reading in the Dewan Rakyat today.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said this step would allow for a more comprehensive review of every clause through engagement with members of parliament from both sides and various stakeholders.
In a statement, Azalina said the move reflected the government's approach to implementing major and complex institutional reforms by shaping balanced and publicly trusted legal frameworks.
"Good reform is reform that is able to endure, strengthen public trust, and continue to function for future generations for the well-being of the people and the nation," she said.
She added that the government had taken note of various views raised by civil society organisations and stakeholders.
Azalina said the bill represented an important step in strengthening transparency, accountability, and good governance, while improving public access to official government information in an orderly and legally grounded manner.
This follows a revelation on June 29 by Prime Minister's Department Legal Affairs Division deputy director-general (policy and development) Datuk Dr S.
Punitha that five states were initially reluctant to support the proposed bill during
stakeholder engagements
.
Punitha said extensive consultations found many state governments and civil servants were hesitant to embrace the legislation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sungai Perak R&R to close temporarily for monkey control operation
Article:
IPOH: The Sungai Perak Rest and Service Area (R&R) northbound will be temporarily closed tomorrow for an operation to address wild monkey disturbances.
PLUS Malaysia Berhad (PLUS), in collaboration with the Sungai Siput Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan), will conduct the operation from 2.30pm to 5pm on Tuesday (July 14).
PLUS said the closure, including the R&R petrol station, was necessary to ensure the safety of highway users, personnel involved and the smooth execution of the operation.
"Throughout the operation, the Sungai Perak R&R (northbound), including the petrol station, will be temporarily closed," it said in a statement on social media.
Motorists have been advised to plan their journeys and use nearby R&Rs or lay-bys as alternatives during the closure.
PLUS said the operation was part of ongoing efforts to provide a safer and more comfortable travel experience for highway users.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Global shares skid as Gulf conflict sends oil surging
Article:
SYDNEY: Share markets sank in Asia on Monday as fighting intensified in the Gulf and Iran claimed to have closed the vital Strait of Hormuz, sending oil prices surging and rekindling inflation risks globally.
The dollar rose with bond yields as investors narrowed the odds of a hike in interest rates from the Federal Reserve, just a day before Chair Kevin Warsh is due to face Congress for the first time in his new role.
Inflation figures for June on Tuesday could show some cooling in the headline rate of 4.2 per cent as petrol prices decline, though some of that will reverse now that oil is rising anew.
Brent crude climbed 4.3 per cent to reach US$79.31 a barrel, up from the recent trough of US$70.14, while U.S. crude added 4.4 per cent to US$74.62 a barrel.
US officials said around 20 vessels had been escorted through the strait in the previous 24 hours, though ship tracking sites showed little traffic moving.
For equity investors, a lot is now riding on the coming earnings season, where expectations are sky-high for AI-related profits. The major banks kick off from Tuesday, while Netflix and General Electric are also on the docket.
"Tech continues to screen highly in our models, supported by stand out earnings growth/momentum and attractive valuations," analysts at Citi wrote in a note.
"While AI volatility may remain elevated over the coming quarter, we maintain our Overweight stance on global IT and the US," they added. "We pair these growth exposures with over weights in cyclical regions/sectors, including Japan, financials and materials."
S&P 500 futures dipped 0.6 per cent while Nasdaq futures lost 1.3 per cent. In Europe, EUROSTOXX 50 futures fell 0.9 per cent, while DAX futures declined 1.0 per cent and FTSE futures eased 0.3 per cent.
Japan's Nikkei shed 2.2 per cent, having lost 1.7 per cent last week, while MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan sank 1.8 per cent.
TESTING THE CHIP BUBBLE
South Korea's formerly red-hot KOSPI sank 7.6 per cent, having already lost almost eight per cent last week, as leveraged bets on semiconductor shares came under pressure.
The market has emerged as a key global barometer for chip-sector sentiment and further losses could ripple out more broadly.
South Korean chipmaker SK Hynix's US-listed shares jumped almost 14 per cent in their Nasdaq debut on Friday. News that Apple had sued OpenAI and two former employees for trade secrets theft emerged after markets closed.
Chip giant Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. reports results on Thursday and another record profit is expected.
Analysts at BofA warned the AI capex boom was eroding cash generation with hyperscalers having spent US$234 billion this year and forward free cash flow expected to turn negative for the first time since at least 2007.
"Against that backdrop, many overlooked areas offer materially better value," they said in a note.
The spike in oil pushed two-year Treasury yields to their highest since early 2025 at 4.2393 per cent, while Fed fund futures slipped two ticks, implying 39 basis points of policy tightening by the end of the year.
That in turn kept the dollar index firm at 101.13. The euro eased a fraction to US$1.1394 as Europe is far more reliant on foreign oil than the US.
The dollar added 0.2 per cent on the yen to 161.97, regaining some of the ground lost on Friday when Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama floated an idea to encourage the US$1.8 trillion Government Pension Investment Fund (GPIF) and other retirement vehicles to bring some of their money home.
"The GPIF currently allocates 50/50 between domestic and offshore and a move back even to the pre-pandemic norm closer to 60/40 would come with a large JPY buying flow," said Taylor Nugent, a senior economist at NAB.
"It is worth noting though that while allocations can theoretically be reviewed any time, they tend to be slow moving, and the FY26 investment plan is already in place."
The pound eased 0.2 per cent to US$1.3383 ahead of a pivotal week in UK politics as Andy Burnham is expected to be formally anointed as Labour leader on Friday and named as prime minister on July 20.
In commodity markets, the rise in yields weighed on non-interest bearing gold, which slipped 1.5 per cent to about US$4,060 an ounce.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kenanga Research keeps 2.9pct jobless forecast, upgrades 2026 GDP outlook
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kenanga Research has maintained its 2026 unemployment rate forecast at 2.9 per cent while raising its gross domestic product (GDP) growth forecast to 4.5-5.0 per cent.
The firm cited resilient labour market conditions and strengthening domestic demand despite external uncertainties.
Kenanga Research said the steady jobs market is expected to support household spending in the second half of the year, underpinned by easing geopolitical tensions, resilient services activity and continued policy support.
Malaysia's unemployment rate remained unchanged at 3.0 per cent in May, while employment expanded 0.1 per cent month-on-month for the fourth consecutive month.
The labour force participation rate also held at a record 70.9 per cent for the fifth straight month.
Kenanga Reserach said hiring continued to be driven by the services sector, particularly wholesale and retail trade, accommodation and food and beverage services, as well as information and communications.
Employment also increased across manufacturing, construction, agriculture, and mining and quarrying.
The firm said the labour market has remained resilient despite concerns over Middle East tensions and their potential impact on global energy prices and supply chains.
However, it noted that labour market tightness has eased slightly.
Perkeso's MYFutureJobs data showed loss of employment cases rose to 8,100 in June from 7,766 in May, above the 2025 monthly average of 6,237, Kenanga Research noted.
At the same time, active job vacancies increased to 109,739 at end-June from 107,439 previously, suggesting employers continue to absorb part of the increase in layoffs even as job placements moderated.
Against this backdrop, Kenanga Research expects domestic demand to remain the key driver of economic growth in the second half, supporting both employment and its upgraded 2026 GDP growth forecast of 4.5-5.0 per cent.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Shipping recovery to take time as Malaysian ports prepare for Hormuz backlog
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Ports, shipping lines and cargo owners are now confronting the formidable task of restoring global supply chains and clearing a backlog of vessels and cargo built up during months of conflict in and around the Strait of Hormuz, a process that industry players say could take weeks or even months.
For Malaysia, the gradual restoration of shipping activity between Asia, the Middle East and Europe could translate into higher vessel traffic and cargo movements through ports along the Strait of Melaka, one of the world's busiest maritime trade routes representing nearly 22 per cent of global trade and 29 per cent of maritime oil.
Three-Phase Recovery Expected
Federation of Malaysian Freight Forwarders (FMFF) president Datuk Dr Tony Chia Han Teun said the sailing of the vessels through Hormuz would likely be done through three phases, whereby energy cargo would be prioritised before gradually restoring container and bulk shipping networks over the coming weeks and months.
"We expect the first phase or initial reopening will almost certainly be given to crude oil tankers, liquefied natural gas (LNG) carriers, refined petroleum product tankers and strategic government-chartered vessels.
"The reason is simple: Hormuz handles roughly 20 per cent of global oil consumption and a significant share of LNG trade. Governments will want energy flows restored first to stabilise markets," he told Bernama.
Based on FMFF conversations with shipping alliances, he said, the other two phases would see backlog clearance as this is where the main congestion occurs and industry assessments suggest that simply clearing the tanker queue could take roughly 10 days to 15 days after reopening.
"At this stage, container vessels begin moving in larger numbers, followed by bulk carriers (transporting grain, minerals, coal) and thereafter general cargo and project cargo vessels would resume scheduled transits.
"Traffic control authorities will likely use convoy-style scheduling or time-slot allocations," Chia said.
He said phase 3 or network recovery is often the part many people overlook as even after ships pass through Hormuz, global shipping schedules remain disrupted.
"A container vessel delayed by two to four weeks in the Gulf may arrive late in Jebel Ali, Port Klang, Singapore, Colombo, Rotterdam and even Hamburg; and shipping lines must then reposition empty containers, rebuild vessel rotations, recover port berthing schedules as well as reorganise crew changes.
"One major liner operator has reportedly estimated around six weeks to restore a fully normal network once vessels can move freely again," he added.
Backlog, Freight Rates To Ease Gradually
Based on public data, more than 102,500 ships passed through the Melaka Strait in 2025.
Today – 135 days since the passage through Hormuz was first restricted Feb 28 – the Strait of Hormuz tracker showed that with about 2,000 vessels stranded due to the West Asia conflict, there were 34 vessels transiting the area with about 250 vessels comprising bulk carriers, tankers and others waiting for their turn.
On whether freight rates would come down, Chia said that rates for the oil tankers are expected to go down quite quickly.
"The war-risk premium and scarcity premium that pushed tanker rates higher should begin easing once insurers gain confidence and vessel queues shrink.
"Expected timeline is initial softening will be up to three weeks and significant decline will be up to two months.
"Container shipping and container freight rates tend to lag. This is as carriers will first try to recover higher fuel costs, war-risk surcharges and schedule disruption costs," he said.
As for bulk shipping, the bulk rates should normalise between the tanker and container markets, he said, adding that this is dependent on confidence that the peace agreement is durable and no new restrictions or attacks occur.
"By October 2026, freight markets and insurance conditions would be close to pre-crisis norms.
"For Malaysia and ASEAN importers and exporters, the first noticeable benefit is likely to be a reduction in fuel-related surcharges and tanker rates within a few weeks, while container freight rates may take one to two months longer to reflect the reopening," Chia added.
Port of Tanjung Pelepas Eyes Recovery
Against this backdrop, Pelabuhan Tanjung Pelepas Sdn Bhd (PTP), which operates the Port of Tanjung Pelepas, remains optimistic about its prospects for the second half (2H) of 2026.
For Malaysia, the Strait of Melaka's corridor includes Port Klang, Tanjung Pelepas, Johor's industrial zones, Penang's electronics ecosystem, LNG infrastructure, power plants, ship repair, bunkering, warehousing, customs systems, and the smaller but vital maintenance firms that keep industrial equipment intact.
PTP chief executive officer Mark Hardiman said any recovery in trade flows is positive for the industry. However, any increase in transshipment activity will depend on how shipping lines restore their networks and schedules.
"The recovery is unlikely to be immediate. Even if the situation stabilises, it will take time for vessels, cargo and schedules to return to normal. PTP expects the process to be gradual over the coming months," he said.
Hardiman also said that while there could be some increase in cargo movements as inventories are replenished and supply chains normalise, the extent of the impact on PTP will depend on cargo routing and shipping line decisions.
Asked on PTP's performance, he said that PTP recorded a strong first half in 2026, supported by continued growth in throughput, high vessel productivity and operational reliability.
"PTP remains optimistic about 2H 2026. While global uncertainties remain, current indicators suggest that 2H 2026 could perform better than the first half, supported by stable customer demand, network growth and continued operational excellence," he added.
Clearing Supply-Chain Bottlenecks to Take Time
Maritime, logistics and supply chain analyst Nazery Khalid concurred that the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz marks only the beginning of the recovery process, with the maritime industry still needing to work through a substantial backlog of vessels and cargo affected by the disruption.
He said the experience from the COVID-19 pandemic illustrates how long supply-chain bottlenecks can take to unwind.
"It took approximately 18 to 24 months for the most severe bottlenecks caused by disruptions to ocean freight flows during the pandemic to be cleared across the global supply chain.
"The initial shock to maritime trade began in early 2020 and supply chains only stabilised by mid-to-late 2023 as trade demand normalised and logistics capacity recovered," he added.
Nazery said key indicators of a full recovery in Gulf-Asia-Europe trade flows would include the restoration of shipping services and cargo volumes to pre-conflict levels, lower insurance costs, declining freight rates and the eventual removal of war-risk classifications in the region.
For Malaysian ports, however, the gradual return of trade flows could prove beneficial, providing sustained cargo activity as shipping lines work through delayed shipments and restore network reliability in the months ahead.
The latest development in West Asia saw both the United States and Iran exchanging attacks after an indirect ceasefire talks ​ended a couple of weeks ago without any public sign of headway toward ​a lasting peace, despite a 60-day ceasefire intended to ⁠create space for diplomacy following the US and Israeli strikes that ​triggered the conflict.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Judge BN on its track record, not politics of hate, says Rauf
Article:
MELAKA: Chief Minister Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh has urged voters to judge Barisan Nasional (BN) based on its track record in developing the state and reject politics driven by hatred and provocation.
Ab Rauf, who is also Melaka BN chairman, said the people should assess the coalition's performance rather than be swayed by slander or divisive rhetoric.
"I hope the democratic process in Melaka will be guided by our track record, not slander, provocation or the politics of hatred, which bring no benefit to the people's harmony.
"I believe the people of Melaka are politically mature enough to recognise that the current state government has delivered positive results for the economy, tourism, investment and social harmony," he told reporters outside the State Legislative Assembly sitting at the Seri Negeri Complex today.
He was commenting on BN's landslide victory in the Johor state election on Saturday, where the coalition retained power with 48 of the 56 state seats.
Asked whether seat negotiations with other parties had begun ahead of the Melaka state election, due this year, Ab Rauf said any announcement would be made "when the time comes".
The current term of the Melaka State Legislative Assembly expires at the end of December.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hormuz traffic slows to multi-week low as renewed US, Iran strikes raise safety risk
Article:
SINGAPORE: The number of vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz fell to multi-week lows on Sunday, shipping data showed, as renewed strikes between the US and Iran and attacks on ships in the Middle East heightened safety concerns.
Six vessels transited the strait on Sunday, ship-tracking data from Kpler showed, the lowest number in five weeks.
Tankers that exited the strait included the Very Large Crude Carrier Humanity, laden with 2 million barrels of Iranian oil and another tanker, Capetan Andreas, carrying about 500,000 barrels of Kuwaiti oil products, the data showed, while three empty tankers entered the Gulf to load oil.
Most of the tankers switched off their transponders when crossing the strait.
There were no liquefied natural gas tankers that entered the strait over the weekend that were visible on ship-tracking data.
One tanker controlled by the Abu Dhabi National Oil Co exited the strait between July 10 and July 12, Kpler data showed.
The vessel is heading for Dahej port in India. U.S. forces completed another wave of strikes against Iran on Sunday, hitting dozens of targets at multiple locations with precision munitions, the Central Command said.
US President Donald Trump said on Sunday that the Strait of Hormuz is open to commercial traffic, although Iran declared earlier that it closed the strait after a vessel travelled on an unapproved route and was struck.
Iran's ​Revolutionary Guards said on Monday that its navy stopped two ships in the Strait of Hormuz last night by shutting down their systems.
It did not name the ships involved.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Psychologist tells Zara inquest child assessment should begin from the womb
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: A child psychologist told the Coroner's Court today that a person's background should be assessed from the time they were in the womb, as an expectant mother's emotional state could influence a child's psychological development.
Dr Noor Aishah Rosli, 51, said studies showed that severe maternal depression during pregnancy could have different effects depending on the child's sex.
"For a mother who is severely depressed, if she is carrying a daughter, the possibility of the child developing depression is very high.
"But if a mother who is severely depressed is carrying a baby boy, the child is more likely to become aggressive," she told coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan on the 79th day of the inquest into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir.
The 76th witness was responding to questions from lawyer Shahlan Jufri, who represents Zara's mother, Noraidah Lamat, on the importance of examining an individual's background from birth.
"I start from the beginning, from the seed. That condition influences a person's development and growth.
"A psychological autopsy means understanding a person's entire life. We cannot look only at the middle or the end," she said.
Noor Aishah said her assessment, which began with Noraidah's pregnancy and Zara's early childhood, found no issues during that period.
She added that her report, which included photographs of Zara celebrating birthdays, receiving awards and taking part in school activities, reflected a cheerful and motivated child.
Asked whether she had followed developments in the inquest, which began in September last year, Noor Aishah said she was only aware of the main reports and had not paid much attention to details or narratives circulating on social media.
The inquest resumes at 2.15pm.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Football critic assaulted, suffers fractured eye socket [WATCH]
Article:
PETALING JAYA: A YouTuber and football critic in his 40s suffered a fractured eye socket and other injuries after he was assaulted by two unidentified men at a petrol station near Bandar Sri Damansara on Saturday evening.
Petaling Jaya district police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said police received a report at 7.56pm.
Preliminary investigations found that the victim was inflating his vehicle's tyres when he was approached by two men on a Yamaha LC motorcycle with an unidentified registration number.
"The suspects repeatedly assaulted the victim before recording a video of the incident and fleeing the scene," he said in a statement today.
The victim suffered a fractured eye socket and several other injuries.
Police said the motive remained under investigation, while efforts to identify and trace the suspects were ongoing.
The case is being investigated under Section 325 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing grievous hurt.
Those with information are urged to contact the nearest police station or investigating officer Inspector Muhammad Danish Noor Azri at 013-6201412.
Earlier, the YouTuber said on X that he was assaulted by two men at a Caltex petrol station in Sri Damansara.
He said he suffered a fractured eye socket and multiple injuries to his body, and had lodged a police report.
"I'm sharing this to inform those who know me and to remind everyone to stay aware of your surroundings and stay safe," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DNB faces three years of losses after share transfer
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Digital Nasional Bhd (DNB) is expected to remain loss-making for three consecutive years after transferring its shares to telecommunications companies (telcos).
CIMB Securities has forecast cumulative net losses of RM1.5 billion between FY26 and FY28, while warning that the share transfer could result in a half-year earnings impact for each participating telco in FY26.
The firm expects DNB to record net losses of RM700 million in FY26, RM500 million in FY27 and RM300 million in FY28.
The Finance Ministry may complete the DNB share transfer to the telcos possibly in the third quarter of 2026, CIMB Securities added.
The proposed share transfer follows the government's move to transition DNB into an industry-owned entity.
Following U Mobile's exit to focus on Malaysia's second 5G network, the remaining participating shareholders comprise CelcomDigi, Maxis and YTL Communications, which will recognise their share of DNB's profits or losses under the equity accounting method.
Meanwhile, the firm said Malaysia's mobile telco industry is expected to record a healthier revenue growth in 2026 and 2027, supported by ongoing market repair and pricing optimisation.
The firm upgraded its forecast for mobile revenue growth in 2026 to two to three per cent from its earlier projection of one per cent, following stronger-than-expected industry developments in the first half of 2026.
"While not spectacular, this would represent the healthiest mobile revenue growth rate since 2013 (with momentum likely to extend into 2027)," it said in a report today.
CIMB Securities estimates every one percentage point uplift in mobile revenue growth will help boost CelcomDigi Bhd's and Maxis Bhd's core net profit by three to five per cent.
It noted that the latter cushions the impact from equity accounting for DNB's net loss, underpinning resilient financial year 2026 (FY26) to FY27 net profit for Maxis and continued growth for CelcomDigi.
CIMB Securities continues to forecast healthy fixed-line revenue growth of between three and five per cent year-on-year in the second half of 2026, supported by stronger performance in both domestic and international wholesale businesses.
However,it said the growth could be moderated by the postponement of the Asia Link Cable's commercial launch to the end of 2026 from the previously expected mid-2026 timeline.
On the cost front, CIMB Securities said the impact from Telekom Malaysia Bhd's staff Prihatin programme in the second half of 2026's may be smaller than expected, with FY26 headline earnings before interest and tax coming in better than its flat year-on-year guidance.
"The review of the mandatory standard on access pricing may start in September; our base case is for a moderate hit to TM's fibre broadband average revenue per user," it said.
CIMB Securities has maintained the sector "Overweight" call with TM as its top pick.
"We also like CelcomDigi (improving FY26 to FY28 earnings outlook, attractive valuation)," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Saarani: Politics not behind delay in Perak-Penang water deal
Article:
IPOH: Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad today denied that political factors were behind the delay in signing the bulk water supply agreement between Perak and Penang.
He said the agreement was a business-to-business (B2B) arrangement between Khazanah Air Perak (KAP) and Penang's water management company, with no involvement from the state government.
"The agreement between Khazanah Air Perak and Penang has nothing to do with the state government because it is a B2B arrangement.
"Khazanah Air Perak is a business entity. It is the party negotiating and signing the agreement with Penang's water management company," he said.
Saarani told reporters this after presenting the 2025 Excellent Service Awards from the State Secretary's Office to 65 recipients at Bangunan Perak Darul Ridzuan today.
He said the agreement was initially scheduled to be signed on July 10 but was postponed at Penang's request.
"I was informed it was supposed to be signed on July 10. However, the Penang government requested a slight postponement, perhaps because there are still matters to be finalised.
"It was Penang that requested the delay, not KAP," he said.
Asked when the agreement would be signed, Saarani said it depended on Penang's readiness.
"Once they are ready, they will sign it," he said.
He reiterated that the delay was not politically motivated.
"No, no, no. It has nothing to do with politics. This is about water," he said.
On the agreed water price, Saarani said details would only be disclosed after the agreement was finalised.
"We cannot announce the price until the agreement is signed. This is a B2B matter," he said.
On June 16, Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said both parties were in the
final stage
of negotiations, with a tentative date fixed for the signing ceremony in Ipoh.
It was reported last year that the Penang-Perak
water project was advancing
following a high-level meeting between the Perbadanan Kemajuan Negeri Perak (PKNP)–Gamuda Berhad Consortium and the Penang government.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zelenskyy orders new PM in major government reshuffle
Article:
KYIV: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced a major reshuffle of the government on Sunday, proposing the replacement of the prime minister and the heads of some law enforcement agencies.
Zelenskyy did not say who would replace incumbent Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko or elaborate on the reasons for the changes.
The reshuffle comes at a pivotal moment in the more than four-year war with Russia, with Moscow escalating its campaign of deadly ballistic missile strikes on Ukraine, and Kyiv planning to domestically produce US-designed Patriot air defence systems.
Zelenskyy did not give an immediate reason for the reshuffle.
In a statement on X, the Ukrainian leader listed a number of tasks that Ukraine needed to undertake, including making progress towards joining the European Union and setting up production of US-designed Patriot air defence systems – a weapons system that the United States has promised Ukraine a license to manufacture.
He also said Ukraine needed to make preparations for winter, following repeated Russian strikes on Ukraine's energy grid each year.
"We determined that these changes require a renewal of the Cabinet of Ministers," he added, without elaborating.
Zelenskyy separately said he planned to assign different people to manage different areas of foreign policy, and that he had offered Svyrydenko a new role leading "relations with a key partner", without elaborating.
Svyrydenko previously represented Ukraine in negotiations with the United States, when the two were working on a minerals and investment deal, although it was not immediately clear if her new role was related to the country.
In a post on X, Svyrydenko thanked Zelenskyy for his trust and said she was "ready to serve the Ukrainian state."
Zelenskyy also proposed "changes among the heads of law enforcement agencies", without going into detail.
Cabinet reshuffles require parliamentary approval – although lawmakers have largely coalesced around Zelenskyy since Russia's invasion, and do not typically block his agenda.
Zelenskyy has not named a successor for Svyrydenko.
Among the potential candidates named in Ukrainian media are Sergiy Koretsky, the CEO of Ukraine's state energy firm Naftogaz, as well as former prime minister Denys Shmygal.
The role of prime minister does not typically include a say on military strategy or frontline operations, where Zelenskyy and his military chiefs call the shots.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nearly 13,000 sit repeat SPM papers from tomorrow
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 12,974 candidates will sit for the 2026 SPM Repeat Examination at 188 examination centres nationwide from July 14 to 16.
The Education Ministry (MoE) said the examination would cover three subjects — Bahasa Melayu, History and Mathematics.
In a statement today, it said 1,530 examination personnel had been appointed to oversee the written papers and Bahasa Melayu Listening Test, while 1,366 personnel would manage the Bahasa Melayu Speaking Test.
The ministry reminded candidates to refer to the examination timetable for details on dates, times, paper codes and instructions.
Candidates were also advised to comply with the standard operating procedures and regulations to ensure the smooth conduct of SPMU 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Petronas to supply LNG to Shizuoka Gas for seven years from 2032
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Petronas LNG Ltd, a subsidiary of Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), has signed a new liquefied natural gas (LNG) sale and purchase agreement (SPA) with Japan's Shizuoka Gas Co Ltd to supply about 0.84 million tonnes of LNG over seven years from 2032.
The agreement extends a partnership spanning three decades and is aimed at strengthening long-term energy security while providing Shizuoka Gas with greater supply flexibility through Petronas' diversified global LNG portfolio.
The SPA was signed by Petronas executive vice-president and chief executive officer of Gas and Maritime Business Datuk Adif Zulkifli and Shizuoka Gas representative director and president Yoshitake Matsumoto.
Adif said the agreement marked more than a commercial milestone, reflecting 30 years of mutual trust and shared growth between the two companies.
"As our customers' needs become increasingly dynamic, Petronas remains committed to delivering reliable and flexible LNG solutions through our global portfolio, supporting long-term energy security while advancing a pragmatic and responsible energy transition," he said in a statement.
The signing ceremony also commemorated the 30th anniversary of Petronas' first LNG cargo delivery to Shizuoka Gas, highlighting a partnership that has evolved alongside Japan's LNG market and changing energy needs.
According to the statement, both companies plan to expand their collaboration beyond the traditional buyer-seller relationship by exploring decarbonisation initiatives across the LNG value chain.
Petronas said it will continue leveraging its integrated global LNG portfolio to provide reliable energy supplies while supporting energy security and a responsible energy transition.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia Airlines partners France's SNCF Voyageurs for seamless air-rail travel
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Airlines has partnered with France's national rail operator SNCF Voyageurs to offer seamless air-rail travel between Paris Charles de Gaulle Airport and 28 destinations across France under a new codeshare agreement.
The partnership, offered through SNCF Voyageurs' Train+Air product, allows passengers flying with Malaysia Airlines to book a single itinerary combining flights and high-speed rail services beyond Paris.
Tickets have been available through the airline's website since July 10.
Under the arrangement, Malaysia Airlines will place its marketing code on SNCF-operated rail services via AccesRail's IATA code "9B", enabling passengers to enjoy coordinated flight and rail connections with a single booking and simplified transfers.
The expanded network includes major French cities such as Lyon, Strasbourg, Rennes, Lille and Marseille.
Malaysia Aviation Group chief executive officer of airline business Bryan Foong said the partnership supports the company's strategy of providing seamless end-to-end connectivity across Europe.
"By integrating air and rail under a unified travel experience, we are extending our network reach beyond Paris Airport to 28 destinations across France, enhancing connectivity while offering customers greater convenience through seamless connections and a single itinerary," he said in a statement.
The collaboration also expands Malaysia Airlines' MHrail initiative, which integrates air and rail services at key international gateways.
This includes Seoul and London Heathrow, as the airline continues to broaden its network through strategic partnerships beyond traditional aviation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: ‘I could have bowed’: Syed Saddiq says six-year ordeal proves principle outweighs fleeting power
Article:
PETALING JAYA, July 13 — Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman walked out of the Federal Court today with his acquittal intact — declaring the fight was about principle, not just personal freedom.
“This is about the principle that truth must be upheld, even when the price is very high. For more than six years I went through this process. I did not run, I did not delay, and I did not ask for special treatment,” he said in an Instagram post.
“Malaysia must be led by those willing to lose position for principle, not sell principle for position. I could have chosen the easier path. I could have stayed silent. I could have bowed.”
He added: “If we ourselves give up when tested, how can we ask the people to keep believing in this country?”
Syed Saddiq stressed his fight was not driven by anger.
“I fought not because I hate anyone. I fought because I love Malaysia too much. This journey nearly robbed me of so much — my time, my good name and my future. But it never robbed me of my faith in Malaysia.”
The former Muar MP said power and position are fleeting, but principles, dignity and service endure. He rejected revenge, saying he left court with responsibility, not resentment.
He thanked his parents, fiancée, family, legal team, friends, constituents in Muar and Malaysians who stood by him throughout the six‑year legal battle.
And after the verdict, he simply noted: he was heading straight to Parliament.

Title: Unicef, NUTP, academics and NGOs all in: Education Ministry goes all-hands-on-deck to tackle student safety crisis
Article:
IPOH, July 13 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) is strengthening efforts to safeguard students’ mental well-being and enhance school safety through a series of preventive and support initiatives.
Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the measures include the establishment of the Special Committee on Educational Institution Safety Reform, comprising stakeholders from within the ministry, academic experts, government agencies and non-governmental organisations (NGOs).
“We are also engaging representatives from organisations such as Unicef and the National Union of the Teaching Profession (NUTP) to gather views and recommendations on how we can improve the current situation while preventing untoward incidents from occurring,” he told reporters.
He was speaking after officiating the Net Energy Metering (NEM) 3.0 solar power generation system, the first pilot project of its kind at a government-aided school (SBK) in Malaysia, at SJK(C) Sam Chai here today.
Wong said the ministry had also intensified ongoing efforts through student mental health screenings, counselling and advisory services, as well as peer support programmes led by guidance and counselling teachers.
He said the ministry treated every incident involving students with the utmost seriousness and had instructed all State Education Departments (JPN) and District Education Offices (PPD) to strictly follow existing guidelines and report cases immediately.
He added that the ministry had strengthened its response mechanism through an official circular (SPI) on SOP137, which outlines procedures for reporting and managing student disciplinary cases.
According to Wong, these efforts are complemented by the Madani Generation Character Development Programme, which aims to nurture students holistically by balancing intellectual, personal and spiritual development.
“Addressing the challenges faced by students requires a comprehensive approach involving all parties, including the MOE, State Education Departments, District Education Offices, schools, parents and the wider community.
“We also urge parents and the community to pay close attention to the well-being and development of children, whether they are at school, outside school or at home, so that together we can ensure they grow up in a healthy, safe and supportive environment,” he said. — Bernama

Title: ‘Cheerful, sociable, active, loving’: In inquest, psychologist says Zara Qairina’s profile challenges suicide theory
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, July 13 — The Coroner’s Court here was told today that Zara Qairina Mahathir did not consistently show signs of severe depression, social withdrawal, hopelessness or suicidal intent before her death.
Child Psychologist Dr Noor Aishah Rosli, 51, who is also a registered counsellor, said the finding was based on an overall assessment of Zara Qairina’s development from birth until she was 13.
Drawing from data analysed for her psychological autopsy report, Dr Noor Aishah, who has about 18 years of experience in clinical child psychology, said there was no clear history of suicide attempts by the teenager before she was found unconscious at her hostel last year.
“In the context of psychological theories related to suicide risk, including the interpersonal model, the assessment must consider whether there is a sense of not being accepted, a feeling of being a burden, the capability to inflict serious self-harm, as well as a combination of acute factors that converge to create risk.
“In Zara’s case, the available data does not strongly indicate that all of these elements were consistently and dominantly present before the incident,” she said while reading her witness statement before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan during the inquest into the teenager’s death.
The 76th witness said the psychological autopsy approach was used to prepare Zara Qairina’s profile, which involved a retrospective forensic psychology assessment of her mental, emotional, social, behavioural, interpersonal and environmental state before her death.
Dr Noor Aishah said the assessment was carried out to determine whether there was psychological evidence suggesting the teenager had the inclination, intent or mental state consistent with taking her own life.
“I also evaluated whether there were psychological, social, family, school environment, peer relationship, hostel experience, personal writings, phone communications and witness information factors that could help the Court understand the full context of Zara’s life before the incident,” she said.
She said the assessment included materials such as photographs, videos, diaries, personal notes, school documents, psychological or psychiatric reports, handwriting analysis reports, forensic document reports, as well as transcripts of phone conversations between Zara Qairina and her mother, Noraidah Lamat.
Dr Noor Aishah said interviews were also conducted with individuals who had direct knowledge of or close relationships with Zara Qairina, including her mother, family members, caregivers, neighbours, school friends, teachers and wardens.
She added that a psychological profile of Noraidah was also developed as she was the person closest to Zara Qairina throughout her life and had deep knowledge of the teenager’s development, interests, routines and experiences.
Dr Noor Aishah said the available data suggested the need to examine external, situational and interpersonal factors in Zara Qairina’s school and hostel environment rather than reaching a conclusion that her death was solely due to family conflict or internal psychological struggles.
She also said Zara Qairina’s relationship with her mother should not be viewed in isolation as the main factor, describing their relationship as one largely marked by attachment, affection, emotional support and open communication.
“In my assessment, it would be inaccurate to draw psychological conclusions based on a single category of material, such as specific writings, without integrating the full range of developmental data, interviews, call transcripts, witness observations, school and family history, protective factors and situational elements close to the time of the incident.
“My professional conclusion is that Zara’s overall psychological profile paints the picture of a cheerful, sociable, active, loving, generous, and motivated child and young adolescent, one with aspirations and a close bond with her mother.
“Although Zara displayed social sensitivity and faced typical adolescent challenges, such as peer conflicts, friendship jealousy, hostel pressures, and some situational maladaptive behaviours, these factors alone are insufficient to support the conclusion that she had a stable or planned intent to take her own life,” she said.
Zara Qairina, 13, died on July 17 at Queen Elizabeth Hospital, where she had been admitted a day earlier after being found unconscious near a drain at her school hostel in Papar at 4am.
The Attorney-General’s Chambers ordered her remains to be exhumed for a post-mortem on Aug 8 before announcing a formal inquest into her death on Aug 13.
The proceedings before Coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan will resume this afternoon. — Bernama

Title: Selangor Sultan rides the LRT3 train, says Shah Alam Line should have been completed earlier, but delay had its ‘wisdom’
Article:
KLANG, July 13 — The Sultan of Selangor today said the Shah Alam LRT3 line should have been completed according to its original schedule, although His Royal Highness added that he believed there was “wisdom” behind the delay.
The remarks came in a statement from the Selangor Royal Office issued after Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah travelled on the newly opened line from Bandar Utama station in Petaling Jaya to Johan Setia station in Klang.
“It would have been preferable if the LRT3 line had been completed according to its original schedule, as I view the rail service as an essential public transport facility for the convenience of the people.”
Sultan Sharafuddin said he was nevertheless grateful the long-awaited rail line had finally become a reality, describing it as a project that would improve the quality of life, ease traffic congestion and provide a more efficient, comfortable and sustainable public transport system.
“I believe there was wisdom behind the delay.”
His Royal Highness also praised the patience of Selangor residents throughout the project's construction and said he looked forward to the completion of the five previously deferred stations — Tropicana, Temasya, Raja Muda, Bukit Raja and Bandar Botanik.
Sultan Sharafuddin also thanked Prasarana Malaysia Berhad, MRCB and everyone involved in delivering the project, noting that more than 13,000 people contributed to its construction over 10 years and nine months.
“While the stations, tracks and trains are the visible outcome of the project, its true success reflects the dedication, professionalism and commitment of more than 13,000 individuals who devoted their time and expertise throughout its construction.”
Sultan Sharafuddin also urged Prasarana to ensure the Shah Alam LRT3 line operates smoothly with minimal disruptions and called on the public to make full use of the service to encourage wider public transport use.

Title: Deputy finance minister pitches one-stop tech financing hub in Penang-Kulim area to power Malaysia’s true ‘Silicon Valley’ dream
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong has proposed that Malaysia establish a one-stop centre for semiconductor and deep-tech financing, offering integrated financing solutions in the heart of the country’s technology cluster.
He said the move would help turn the technology cluster into Malaysia’s true Silicon Valley by bridging the gap between deep tech and capital.
“Our ambition is for Penang and its neighbouring Kulim to take their place alongside Silicon Valley, Taiwan’s Hsinchu, and the Netherlands’ Brainport Eindhoven as a premier global tech cluster,” he said.
Liew said the one-stop centre could support themes, and not only single names, inviting investors to back a well-governed national story built on trust in Malaysia’s regulatory framework.
“Therefore, I would like to invite industry and capital market players and regulators to share your thoughts on this,” he said in his opening address at The Edge-HSBC E&E Symposium 2026: The Value Chain Shift, here today.
Liew also called on the capital market to view Malaysia as a technology nation rather than just a trading nation and to take the country’s tech stocks more seriously.
“Therefore, a warm invitation to the venture and private capital community to come upstream and onwards with us, into design and intellectual property, where the greatest value is created.
“To our market institutions, help (us) deepen liquidity, extend ratings coverage, and fuel the growth of this (semiconductor) sector,” he added.
According to Liew, electrical and electronics (E&E) products account for 40 per cent of Malaysia’s national exports, the largest share of any category.
He said that, apart from China and Taiwan, around 13 per cent of global semiconductor assembly and testing is carried out in Malaysia. — Bernama

Title: Singapore president gets 21-gun salute at Istana Negara in first Malaysia state visit
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — Singaporean President Tharman Shanmugaratnam was accorded a state welcoming ceremony at Istana Negara today, marking his first state visit to Malaysia since assuming office in September 2023.
President Tharman and his wife, Jane Ittogi Shanmugaratnam, arrived at Istana Negara at 10am and were received by His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia.
Also in attendance were Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail; Deputy Prime Ministers Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof; as well as Cabinet ministers.
The welcoming ceremony at the parade ground began with the playing of the national anthems of Singapore and Malaysia by the Central Band of the Royal Malay Regiment, followed by a 21-gun salute.
President Tharman then inspected a guard of honour mounted by four officers and 103 personnel from the First Battalion of the Royal Malay Regiment, led by Major Mohd Masrur Hidayat Masri.
President Tharman is undertaking a three-day state visit to Malaysia from July 13 to 15 at the invitation of the King.
According to the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the visit reciprocates Sultan Ibrahim’s state visit to Singapore on May 6 and 7, 2024, and reflects the close and longstanding bilateral ties between the two neighbouring countries.
The ministry noted that Singapore remains Malaysia’s second-largest trading partner globally and its largest within Asean.
Bilateral trade between Malaysia and Singapore totalled RM402.35 billion (US$93.97 billion) in 2025, an increase of 1.5 per cent compared with 2024.
Trade continued to grow this year, rising 14.5 per cent to RM189.90 billion (US$47.87 billion) between January and May 2026, compared with RM165.93 billion (US$37.65 billion) during the corresponding period in 2025. — Bernama

Title: Putrajaya lines up RM207m projects to spur Pasir Puteh’s ECRL-linked growth
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 13 — The government has approved 46 projects for the Pasir Puteh parliamentary constituency in Kelantan for 2026, with a total allocation ceiling of RM207.2 million, including downstream industrial infrastructure to support activities near the East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) cargo station.
